claims.name: lambda0_lower_bound_mu2_positive
claims.detail: lambda0 := min_k K(k) >= mu^2 = 0.005 > 0 (K(k)>=mu^2 for all k; continuum inf = 0.005 = mu^2 is the SHARP lower bound; on a generic lattice lambda0 = 0.00857298 > mu^2, equality NOT generic). Only lambda0>0 is used for sectoriality; the H^2 equivalence uses K(k)>=mu^2 and K(k) ~ (1+|k|^2)^2

claims.name: sobolev_linf_threshold
claims.detail: H^s(T^3)->L^inf for s>1.5; fractional-power exponent threshold beta>0.375=3/8

claims.name: sobolev_L10_threshold
claims.detail: H^s->L^10 for s>=1.2000000000000002=6/5 (so phi^5 in L^2); below the L^inf threshold 1.5, so s>3/2 is the binding requirement

claims.name: smoothing_exponent_finite_beta_0.375
claims.detail: sup_x x^0.375 e^-x = 0.47577 (= beta^beta e^-beta, attained x=beta); ||L^0.375 e^-tL|| <= C t^-0.375, C=0.47577

claims.name: smoothing_exponent_finite_beta_0.450
claims.detail: sup_x x^0.450 e^-x = 0.44516 (= beta^beta e^-beta, attained x=beta); ||L^0.450 e^-tL|| <= C t^-0.450, C=0.44516

claims.name: smoothing_exponent_finite_beta_0.500
claims.detail: sup_x x^0.500 e^-x = 0.42888 (= beta^beta e^-beta, attained x=beta); ||L^0.500 e^-tL|| <= C t^-0.500, C=0.42888

claims.name: sextic_dominates_quartic_coercive
claims.detail: gamma=1.62>0; max_t[(|lam|/4)t^4-(gamma/12)t^6] = C_* = 0.0100984 (at t^2=0.5309); => (|lam|/4)t^4 <= (gamma/12)t^6 + C_*, F coercive + bounded below

claims.name: continuation_window_nonempty
claims.detail: beta in (0.375,0.5]: X^beta=H^(4beta), 4beta in (1.5,2]; since 4beta<=2, H^2 SUBSET X^beta so the energy H^2 bound controls the X^beta norm => global continuation (no finite-time blow-up)
